lab.title: Variables & Types
lab.intro: In this lab, we will explore the basics of variables and types in Python. You will learn how to declare variables, use different data types, and practice with various operators.
lab.tutor_opening: Hello, let's get started with variables and types in Python. This lab is designed to help you understand the basics of variables, data types, and operators. You will have hands-on practice with various examples and tasks.

cells.title: Introduction to Variables
cells.narrative: A variable is a named box that holds a value. You can think of it as a labeled box where you can store a value. In Python, you can declare a variable using the assignment operator (=). For example: x = 5
cells.tutor_script: Let's start with the basics. A variable is a named box that holds a value. You can declare a variable using the assignment operator (=). Try running this code to see how it works.

cells.title: Data Types
cells.narrative: Python has several built-in data types, including integers, floats, and strings. You can use the type() function to check the data type of a variable.
cells.code:
x = 5
y = 3.14
z = 'hello'
print(type(x))
print(type(y))
print(type(z))
cells.tutor_script: Now, let's explore the different data types in Python. You can use the type() function to check the data type of a variable. Try running this code to see how it works.

cells.title: Variable Declaration
cells.narrative: Declare three variables: x, y, and z. Assign x an integer value, y a float value, and z a string value.
cells.task_prompt: Declare three variables: x, y, and z. Assign x an integer value, y a float value, and z a string value.
cells.tutor_script: Now it's your turn to practice. Declare three variables: x, y, and z. Assign x an integer value, y a float value, and z a string value. Use the type() function to check the data type of each variable.
tips: Use the assignment operator (=) to assign a value to a variable.
Use the type() function to check the data type of a variable.

cells.title: Operator Practice
cells.narrative: Use the modulus operator (%) to find the remainder of 17 divided by 5.
cells.task_prompt: Use the modulus operator (%) to find the remainder of 17 divided by 5.
cells.tutor_script: Now, let's practice using the modulus operator (%). Use the modulus operator (%) to find the remainder of 17 divided by 5.
tips: The modulus operator (%) returns the remainder of the division.
Use the print() function to print the result.

cells.title: Conclusion
cells.narrative: In this lab, you learned about variables and data types in Python. You practiced declaring variables, using different data types, and working with operators.
cells.tutor_script: Congratulations! You have completed the lab. You now have a good understanding of variables and data types in Python. Keep practicing and you will become proficient in no time.

lab.completion_message: Congratulations! You have completed the Variables & Types lab. You now have a good understanding of variables and data types in Python. Keep practicing and you will become proficient in no time.
